descriptions.description: The AN/APG-81 is an active electronically scanned array (AESA) fire-control radar developed for the Lockheed Martin F-35 Lightning II. Designed by Northrop Grumman, the APG-81 provides all-weather air-to-air and air-to-ground target acquisition and tracking for all F-35 variants. The radar's advanced signal processing and electronic steering capability enable simultaneous multi-target engagement while maintaining a low probability of intercept (LPI) signature.
descriptions.shortDescription: AESA fire-control radar for the F-35 with integrated air-to-air and air-to-ground capability.

descriptions.description: The APG-81 employs a flat active electronically scanned array (AESA) antenna integrated into the F-35's nose. The antenna uses thousands of small phased-array elements to electronically steer the beam without mechanical movement, enabling rapid target acquisition across a wide search volume. The radar operates in X-band and uses advanced algorithms for track-while-scan (TWS) and synthetic aperture radar (SAR) ground mapping. Integration with the F-35's sensor fusion system allows the radar to coordinate detections with infrared and electronic warfare sensors.
descriptions.shortDescription: Flat AESA antenna with electronic beam steering and integrated sensor fusion.
